rationale: This reaction shows the asymmetric hydrogenation of a simple prochiral ketone, acetophenone, to a chiral secondary alcohol, (R)-1-phenylethanol. This is a classic textbook example of the Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation, demonstrating the high enantioselectivity achieved with a Ru-BINAP catalyst system. The substrate and product are simple, acyclic at the reaction center, and clearly illustrate the transformation of a carbonyl group to a chiral alcohol.
collision_avoidance_note: The chosen example is the hydrogenation of a simple ketone, a core application of the Noyori reaction. It avoids complex polycyclic structures, functional groups prone to side reactions, or motifs that could overlap with other named reactions like Meerwein-Ponndorf-Verley (which uses an aluminum alkoxide catalyst and an alcohol as the hydrogen source) or Luche reduction (which uses NaBH4/CeCl3). The focus is specifically on the Ru-BINAP catalyzed asymmetric addition of hydrogen to a carbonyl.
